title: 4th of July Parade & Celebration
description: Alameda's beloved annual Fourth of July parade down Park Street — one of the largest in the Bay Area. Live bands, floats, and fireworks at the lagoon at dusk.

title: Soul of Our Nation Art Opening
description: Opening reception for a group exhibition celebrating Black American artistic heritage. Live music, spoken word, and community gathering at Rhythmix.

title: Rhythmix in the Parks: Sekhou Kouyate & Senegal
description: Free outdoor concert featuring Sekhou Kouyate and his ensemble bringing West African rhythms and kora to Alameda's Washington Park. Bring blankets and snacks.

title: Downtown Alameda Art & Wine Faire
description: Annual street fair on Park Street with local artists, live music stages, food vendors, and wine tasting. Free admission, all ages welcome.

title: Pour Your HeART Out: Community Mural Celebration
description: Unveiling of a new community-painted mural at Rhythmix, followed by live music, food trucks, and a community celebration of public art in Alameda.

title: Marcus King Band
description: South Carolina's Marcus King brings his soulful, psychedelic blues-rock to the Fox. Expect scorching guitar solos, gospel roots, and a full-throttle band.

title: Widespread Panic
description: Athens, Georgia jam legends Widespread Panic return for two nights at the Fox. Extended setlists, rotating jams, and the kind of crowd that hasn't stopped dancing since 1986.

title: Poppy
description: Genre-blurring performer Poppy brings her experimental metal-meets-pop spectacle to the Fox. Elaborate staging, unexpected sonic swerves, and a devoted crowd.

title: Peter Rowan & The Free Mexican Air Force / Rex Foundation Benefit
description: A special Rex Foundation benefit featuring bluegrass legend Peter Rowan and The Free Mexican Air Force. Outdoor summer night at Berkeley's iconic amphitheater.

title: Lukas Nelson & Promise of the Real
description: Willie's son Lukas Nelson leads his powerhouse band through Americana, country-rock, and originals. One of the tightest live acts going, with roots you can feel.

title: Bartees Strange
description: Oklahoma-born indie rock shapeshifter Bartees Strange plays the Greek on his summer tour. Expect moving, maximalist anthems that fold in R&B, emo, and folk.

title: The Woggles + Hot Laundry
description: Sweaty garage-punk double bill at Bottom of the Hill. The Woggles have been ripping through venues for decades; Hot Laundry is the local opener you won't regret catching.

title: Widowspeak + Dead Gowns
description: Slowburn indie dream-pop from Brooklyn's Widowspeak, supported by Dead Gowns. Hazy guitars, whispered vocals, and the kind of room where you nurse one drink all night.

title: PLOSIVS / Tijuana Panthers
description: San Diego punk supergroup PLOSIVS (featuring members of Drive Like Jehu & Hot Snakes) co-headlines with Tijuana Panthers. A classic Bottom of the Hill Saturday night.
